---
title: "Welcome to the College of Idaho!"
subtitle: "Data Science Activity"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    logo: style/Howler-Logo-01.png 
    css: styles.css
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    center: false
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "style/slide_images/image2.jpeg"
      data-background-opacity: "0.6"
      data-background-color: "#000000"
---

## Who Am I?

::: incremental
- Eric Friedlander, Assistant Professor of Computer Science
- What is a Professor?
- Professors have two jobs... Teaching and Research
- My education:
  - B.S. in Mathematics and Statistics at Rice University
  - Ph.D. in Statistics and Operations Research at UNC-Chapel Hill
- What is a Ph.D.?
:::




## {background-image="phd_images/slide-01.png?v=2" background-size="101%" background-position="center"}

## Imagine a circle that contains all of human knowledge:

![](phd_images/slide-02.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## By the time you finish elementary school, you know a little:



![](phd_images/slide-03.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## By the time you finish high school, you know a bit more:

![](phd_images/slide-04.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## With a bachelor's degree, you gain a specialty:

![](phd_images/slide-05.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## A master's degree deepens that specialty:

![](phd_images/slide-06.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## Reading research papers takes you to the edge of human knowledge:

![](phd_images/slide-07.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## Once you're at the boundary, you focus:

![](phd_images/slide-08.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## You push at the boundary for a few years:

![](phd_images/slide-09.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## Until one day, the boundary gives way:

![](phd_images/slide-10.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## And, that dent you've made is called a Ph.D.:

![](phd_images/slide-11.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## Of course, the world looks different to you now:

![](phd_images/slide-12.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## So, don't forget the bigger picture:



![](phd_images/slide-13.png?v=2){.phd-image}

##

![](phd_images/slide-14.png?v=2){.phd-image}

##

![](phd_images/slide-15.png?v=2){.phd-image}

## My Ph.D.

::: incremental
- Dissertation: Diffusion Approximations for Weakly Interacting Particle Systems
- BORING!
  ![](style/slide_images/eli.jpeg){.absolute .fragment .fade-in-then-out top=100 left=50 width="800"}
- OK OK OK OK OK... I studied queueing...
- What is queueing?
- How things line up...
- STILL BORING!!!
:::

## Lot's of cool things form lines

- Computers
- Servers
- Hospitals
- Ubers
- Banks
- Airlines

## Activity Time!!!

::: incremental
- How can we use Math to improve our response to emergency situations?
- Our setting for today: THERE'S BEEN A MAJOR GAS LEAK IN THE LIBRARY AT THE COLLEGE OF IDAHO!
- About 50 patients with a variety of symptoms
- Only 10 ambulances (although more are on the way)
- How can we prioritize who should get in the ambulance first?
:::

## Our Job

- Develop a **triage** policy
- Based on a patients symptoms classify them as:
  - "Red": Highest priority for an ambulance
  - "Yellow": Medium priority for an ambulance
  - "Green": Lowest priority for an ambulance
- If a patient has to wait too long for an ambulance, they require a life-flight (very expensive and not good for the patient)

## How can we do this?

::: incremental
- I've build a model?
- What's a model?
- [I've made it into an app](https://efriedlander.shinyapps.io/high-school-triage-activity/)
:::

# Let's walk through the app together

## Rest of time

- Who can design the **best** triage policy!
